pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk into the kitchen and i forget for a second that he isn't there, and then it hits me all over again. the house feels like it's empty, even when i'm in it. i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed or making coffee anymore. it feels like i'm just a ghost going through the motions of a life that doesn't even belong to me anymore.

pool.baseline: i cant stop thinking about how none of this actually matters. like, if you look at the scale of the universe, everything we do is just a tiny blip of noise that’s gonna be deleted eventually. it feels like we're just pretending there's a point to any of this struggle. is it just me or is the whole concept of "living" kind of a losing battle?

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to nothing but bitter wax and shadows, much like my heart. Every word he spoke was a dagger, and every promise he made was a lie that has left me hollow. I feel as though I am merely a ghost haunting my own life, drifting through these cold, empty halls... there is no light left in this world for me, only the quiet, beckoning dark. It would be so much more peaceful to simply fade away, wouldn't it? To let the night take what is left of me.
